title: Mercer Benchmark Database (MBD)
pricingModel: Annual subscription; tiered by # of benchmark jobs selected
jobFamilies: Accounting & Finance, Administrative, Engineering, HR, IT, Legal, Manufacturing, Marketing, Operations, Sales, Supply Chain, and more
orgSizeRange: All sizes — small, mid, large employers
countriesRegions: US, Canada, UK, Europe, APAC, LATAM, MEA
metroCuts: Yes — US metro, state, and regional cuts available
industryCuts: Yes — 20+ industry cuts including Technology, Financial Services, Healthcare, Manufacturing, Energy, Retail
bestFor: Large employers needing broad, cross-industry data across multiple geographies and job families
notes: One of the largest and most widely used compensation databases globally. Modular design lets you purchase only the job families you need. Excellent for global benchmarking.

title: Radford Global Technology Survey
pricingModel: Annual subscription; priced by company revenue tier
participationDiscount: Yes — participation required for best pricing
jobFamilies: Software Engineering, Hardware Engineering, Data Science, Product Management, Design, IT, Sales, Marketing, G&A, Clinical, Regulatory
jobLevels: Entry through SVP; individual contributor and management tracks
orgSizeRange: Pre-revenue startups through large public companies
countriesRegions: US, Canada, UK, Ireland, Germany, India, China, Japan, Singapore, Australia, Israel
metroCuts: Yes — Bay Area, Seattle, NYC, Austin, Boston, and 30+ US metros
industryCuts: Yes — SaaS, Hardware, Biotech, Fintech, Gaming, Semiconductor, E-Commerce
deliveryFormat: Online platform (Radford Network), Excel, custom cuts
bestFor: Technology companies of all sizes needing deep tech job architecture and equity benchmarking
notes: The gold standard for tech compensation. Unmatched depth in equity data (stock options, RSUs, refresh grants). Critical for any tech company competing for engineering talent.

title: Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS)
jobFamilies: All SOC major occupation groups — Management, Business & Financial, Computer & Mathematical, Architecture & Engineering, Healthcare, Legal, Education, Arts, and more
jobLevels: Occupation-level only (no individual contributor vs. manager distinction)
numIncumbents: Coverage of ~130 million wage & salary workers
metroCuts: Yes — 400+ metro areas, all 50 states, national
deliveryFormat: Downloadable files (Excel, CSV), online search tool
bestFor: Baseline market data, geographic differentials, budget-conscious organizations needing free and publicly defensible data
notes: Essential free resource for any comp professional. Extremely broad coverage but limited to base wages only. Great for geographic pay analysis and as a starting point. Pair with industry-specific surveys for a complete picture.

title: CUPA-HR Administrators in Higher Education Survey
pricingModel: Included with CUPA-HR membership; non-member pricing available
participationDiscount: Free with membership; participation strongly encouraged
jobFamilies: Academic Affairs, Administrative Affairs, Business & Finance, Development/Advancement, Enrollment Management, Facilities, Human Resources, Information Technology, Legal, Library, Research Administration, Student Affairs
orgSizeRange: Small colleges to large research universities
industryCuts: By Carnegie classification, institution type (public/private), enrollment size
deliveryFormat: Online interactive tool (DataOnDemand), PDF reports, Excel
bestFor: Colleges and universities benchmarking administrator and executive compensation
notes: The definitive source for higher education compensation data. Covers administrators, faculty, and professionals in separate survey modules. Membership-based pricing makes it very affordable for institutions. Essential for any higher ed HR/comp team.

title: MGMA Provider Compensation Data
pricingModel: Annual subscription; member vs. non-member pricing
participationDiscount: Yes — free access for participants; reduced rate for MGMA members
jobFamilies: All major medical specialties — Primary Care, Surgery, Cardiology, Oncology, Radiology, Anesthesiology, Psychiatry, Pediatrics, OB/GYN, Emergency Medicine, and many more
orgSizeRange: Solo practices to large health systems
industryCuts: By practice type (physician-owned, hospital-owned), group size, academic vs. non-academic
deliveryFormat: Online interactive platform (MGMA DataDive), PDF reports, Excel
dataLag: ~6 months (reports on prior calendar year data)
bestFor: Healthcare organizations benchmarking physician and advanced practice provider compensation
notes: The gold standard for physician and APP compensation data. Widely cited in healthcare compensation discussions and used by the majority of health systems. Includes productivity metrics (wRVUs) alongside compensation data, which is unique and essential for healthcare pay-for-performance models.
